content.problemStatement:
You are given:


positive integers NNN and KKK (K‚Č§N)(K \le N)(K‚Č§N).
an array AAA of size NNN, where each element of the array is a **binary** string of length KKK;
For each 1‚Č§j<2K1\le j\lt 2^K1‚Č§j<2K, your task is to find whether there exists a set of indices {i1,i2,‚Ä¶,im}\{i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_m\}{i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,im‚Äč} (1‚Č§ij,m‚Č§N)(1\le i_j, m \le N)(1‚Č§ij‚Äč,m‚Č§N) such that:


The decimal value of (Ai1(A_{i_1}(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ Ai2A_{i_2}Ai2‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ Ai3A_{i_3}Ai3‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ ‚ą£|‚ą£ Aim)A_{i_m})Aim‚Äč‚Äč) equals jjj, where ‚ą£|‚ą£ denotes bitwise OR operation.
Print `YES` if a set of indices satisfying the given condition exists for **all** 1‚Č§j<2K1\le j\lt 2^K1‚Č§j<2K. Otherwise, print `NO`.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.
The iii-th of the next NNN lines contains the binary string AiA_iAi‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print `YES` if a set of indices satisfying the given condition exists for **all** 1‚Č§j<2K1\le j\lt 2^K1‚Č§j<2K. Otherwise, print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500500500.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

j=1j = 1j=1 : The set of indices {1}\{1\}{1} satisfies the condition as decimal value of A1=1=jA_1 = 1 = jA1‚Äč=1=j.
j=2j = 2j=2 : The set of indices {2}\{2\}{2} satisfies the condition as decimal value of A2=2=jA_2 = 2 = jA2‚Äč=2=j.
j=3j = 3j=3 : The set of indices {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2} satisfies the condition as decimal value of (A1(A_1(A1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A2)=3=jA_2) = 3 = jA2‚Äč)=3=j.

Test case 222: The set of indices for j=2,3,4,6,7j = 2, 3, 4, 6, 7j=2,3,4,6,7 are {2},{3},{4},{2,4},\{2\}, \{3\}, \{4\}, \{2,4\},{2},{3},{4},{2,4}, and {3,4}\{3, 4\}{3,4} respectively.
However, there doesn't exist any set of indices for j=1j=1j=1 and j=5j=5j=5. Hence output is NO.
